Name: training_free_adaptive_routing
Title: Training-Free Adaptive Routing for Optimal Model Performance Estimation in Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: Can we achieve a rigorous estimation of model performance using a training-free adaptive routing framework that leverages geometric representation and curvature-aware diagnostics?
Related Work: While recent works like NEAR focus on zero-cost proxies for model performance estimation, they often lack the formalized curvature diagnostics and robust representation geometry that can isolate algorithmic pathologies. This proposal distinguishes itself by integrating projection-based mechanistic probes with curvature-aware optimization, providing a more comprehensive understanding of model performance without requiring extensive training.
Abstract: In this proposal, we aim to develop a training-free adaptive routing framework for optimal model performance estimation in machine learning. Existing methods largely rely on training-based evaluations or zero-cost proxies that do not fully account for underlying geometric structures. Our approach integrates curvature-aware diagnostics with projection-based mechanistic probes, enabling a training-free evaluation of model performance. By leveraging the principles of representation geometry, we can isolate algorithmic pathologies and quantify performance metrics such as gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning. We will empirically validate our framework through a series of experiments that assess model performance across various architectures and tasks, ensuring adherence to strict computational overhead bounds. Our findings will foster a deeper understanding of model behavior, bridging theoretical insights with practical applications.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the curvature diagnostics of various neural network architectures using projection-based probes. Metrics: Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay.

Experiment 2: Implement the proposed training-free adaptive routing on benchmark datasets, measuring the performance against traditional training-based approaches. Metrics: performance accuracy, computational overhead, and generalization capabilities.
Experiment 3: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the effects of curvature-aware diagnostics on model performance estimation. Metrics: performance variance and confidence intervals.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may face challenges in accurately capturing the complexities of certain model behaviors, especially in highly non-linear contexts.
There is a risk that the performance metrics derived may not fully generalize across all types of machine learning tasks or architectures.
The reliance on geometric representations may introduce computational overhead that could limit scalability in larger models.
